name: Reverse Linked List
description: Given the head of a singly linked list, reverse the list and return the reversed list.
constraints: The number of nodes is in [0, 5000]. -5000 <= Node.val <= 5000.

name: Reverse a Doubly Linked List
description: Given the head of a doubly linked list, reverse the list and return the new head. Each node has val, next, and prev pointers.
constraints: The number of nodes is in [0, 10^4]. -10^5 <= Node.val <= 10^5.

name: Middle of the Linked List
description: Given the head of a singly linked list, return the middle node. If there are two middle nodes, return the second middle node.
constraints: The number of nodes is in [1, 100]. 1 <= Node.val <= 100.

name: Merge Two Sorted Lists
description: Given the heads of two sorted linked lists, merge them into one sorted list by splicing together the nodes. Return the head of the merged list.
constraints: Both lists are sorted in non-decreasing order. The number of nodes in both lists is in [0, 50]. -100 <= Node.val <= 100.

name: Linked List Cycle
description: Given head of a linked list, determine if it has a cycle. A cycle exists if some node can be reached again by continuously following the next pointer. Use Floyd's tortoise and hare algorithm.

examples.input: head = [3,2,0,-4], pos = 1 (tail connects to node index 1)

constraints: The number of nodes is in [0, 10^4]. -10^5 <= Node.val <= 10^5. pos is -1 or a valid index.

name: Linked List Cycle II
description: Given the head of a linked list, return the node where the cycle begins. If there is no cycle, return null. Use Floyd's algorithm: after slow and fast meet, reset one pointer to head and advance both by one until they meet at the cycle start.

examples.output: Node with value 2 (cycle starts at index 1)

constraints: The number of nodes is in [0, 10^4]. -10^5 <= Node.val <= 10^5.

name: Length of Cycle in Linked List
description: Given a linked list that may contain a cycle, find the length of the cycle. If no cycle exists, return 0. Use Floyd's algorithm to find the meeting point, then count nodes in the cycle.
constraints: The number of nodes is in [0, 10^4]. -10^5 <= Node.val <= 10^5.

name: Palindrome Linked List
description: Given the head of a singly linked list, return true if it is a palindrome. Can you do it in O(n) time and O(1) space? Hint: find middle, reverse second half, compare.
constraints: The number of nodes is in [1, 10^5]. 0 <= Node.val <= 9.

name: Intersection of Two Linked Lists
description: Given the heads of two singly linked lists, return the node at which they intersect. If no intersection, return null. The lists do not have cycles. After function returns, lists must retain their original structure.
constraints: listA has m nodes. listB has n nodes. 1 <= m, n <= 3 * 10^4. 1 <= Node.val <= 10^5.

name: Remove Nth Node From End of List
description: Given the head of a linked list, remove the nth node from the end and return the head. Use two-pointer technique with n gap.
constraints: The number of nodes is sz. 1 <= sz <= 30. 0 <= Node.val <= 100. 1 <= n <= sz.

name: Add Two Numbers
description: Given two non-empty linked lists representing two non-negative integers stored in reverse order (each node is a single digit), add the two numbers and return the sum as a linked list.
constraints: The number of nodes in each list is in [1, 100]. 0 <= Node.val <= 9. Numbers do not contain leading zeros except the number 0 itself.

name: Odd Even Linked List
description: Given the head of a singly linked list, group all odd-indexed nodes together followed by all even-indexed nodes. The first node is odd, the second is even, and so on. Do it in O(1) extra space and O(n) time.
constraints: The number of nodes is in [0, 10^4]. -10^6 <= Node.val <= 10^6.

name: Sort List
description: Given the head of a linked list, sort it in ascending order in O(n log n) time and O(1) space. Use merge sort: find middle, split, recursively sort, merge.
constraints: The number of nodes is in [0, 5 * 10^4]. -10^5 <= Node.val <= 10^5.

name: Reorder List
description: Given a singly linked list L0 -> L1 -> ... -> Ln-1 -> Ln, reorder it to L0 -> Ln -> L1 -> Ln-1 -> L2 -> Ln-2 -> ... Do it in-place. Hint: find middle, reverse second half, merge alternately.
constraints: The number of nodes is in [1, 5 * 10^4]. 1 <= Node.val <= 1000.

name: Rotate List
description: Given the head of a linked list, rotate the list to the right by k places.
constraints: The number of nodes is in [0, 500]. -100 <= Node.val <= 100. 0 <= k <= 2 * 10^9.

name: Copy List with Random Pointer
description: Given a linked list where each node has a next pointer and a random pointer (which can point to any node or null), construct a deep copy of the list. Return the head of the copied list.
constraints: 0 <= n <= 1000. -10^4 <= Node.val <= 10^4. Node.random is null or points to a node in the list.

name: Find the Duplicate Number
description: Given an array of n + 1 integers where each integer is in [1, n], there is exactly one repeated number. Find it without modifying the array and using O(1) extra space. Hint: treat indices as linked list pointers and use Floyd's cycle detection.
constraints: 1 <= n <= 10^5. nums.length == n + 1. 1 <= nums[i] <= n. There is only one repeated number.
python_template:
# Find the Duplicate Number

from typing import List

def findDuplicate(nums: List[int]) -> int:
    pass

name: Flatten a Multilevel Doubly Linked List
description: Given a doubly linked list where some nodes have a child pointer to a separate doubly linked list, flatten all lists into a single-level doubly linked list. Child lists should be inserted between the node and its next.
constraints: The number of nodes is at most 1000. 1 <= Node.val <= 10^5.

name: Flatten a Linked List with Next and Bottom Pointers
description: Given a linked list where every node has two pointers: next (right) and bottom (down). All bottom lists are sorted. Merge all lists into a single sorted list using the bottom pointer. Return the head.
constraints: Number of nodes <= 500. 1 <= Node.val <= 1000. All bottom lists are sorted.

name: LRU Cache
description: Design a data structure that follows the constraints of a Least Recently Used (LRU) cache. Implement get(key) and put(key, value). Both must run in O(1) time. When the cache reaches capacity, evict the least recently used key before inserting a new one.
constraints: 1 <= capacity <= 3000. 0 <= key <= 10^4. 0 <= value <= 10^5. At most 2 * 10^5 calls to get and put.

name: Insert Node in Doubly Linked List
description: Given a doubly linked list and a position, insert a new node with a given value at the specified position. Handle insertion at head, tail, and middle.
constraints: 0 <= pos <= length of DLL. -10^5 <= val <= 10^5.

name: Remove Duplicates from Sorted Doubly Linked List
description: Given a sorted doubly linked list, remove all duplicate nodes so that each value appears only once. Return the modified list.
constraints: The number of nodes is in [0, 300]. -100 <= Node.val <= 100. The list is sorted.

name: Delete Node in a Doubly Linked List
description: Given a doubly linked list and a reference to a node to be deleted, delete the node. Handle head, tail, and middle cases. Update prev and next pointers accordingly.
constraints: The number of nodes is in [1, 10^4]. -10^5 <= Node.val <= 10^5.

name: Find Pairs with Given Sum in Sorted DLL
description: Given a sorted doubly linked list and a target sum, find all pairs of nodes whose values add up to the target. Use two pointers from both ends.
constraints: 1 <= number of nodes <= 10^4. 1 <= Node.val <= 10^4. List is sorted in ascending order.

name: Insert Greatest Common Divisors in Linked List
description: Given the head of a linked list, insert a new node with the GCD of every pair of adjacent nodes between them. Return the modified list.
constraints: The number of nodes is in [1, 5000]. 1 <= Node.val <= 1000.

name: Split Linked List in Parts
description: Given the head of a linked list and an integer k, split the list into k consecutive parts. Parts should be as equal in size as possible — earlier parts should be at most one node larger. Parts that have no nodes should be null.
constraints: The number of nodes is in [0, 1000]. 0 <= Node.val <= 1000. 1 <= k <= 50.

name: Merge K Sorted Lists
description: Given an array of k linked lists, each sorted in ascending order, merge all into one sorted linked list and return it. Use a min-heap or divide-and-conquer.
constraints: k == lists.length. 0 <= k <= 10^4. 0 <= lists[i].length <= 500. -10^4 <= lists[i][j] <= 10^4. Each list is sorted in ascending order. Sum of all lists[i].length <= 10^4.

name: Reverse Nodes in k-Group
description: Given the head of a linked list, reverse the nodes k at a time and return the modified list. If the number of remaining nodes is less than k, leave them as-is. You may not alter the values, only the nodes.
constraints: The number of nodes is n. 1 <= k <= n <= 5000. 0 <= Node.val <= 1000.
